title: Data dictionary: columns, tables, and AEMO source
text: Timezone. settlementdate timestamp columns POSIXct "Australia/Brisbane" (AEST, UTC+10, daylight saving). See vignette("timestamps") details. Period-ending. row stamped 14:05:00 covers interval ending 14:05 (.e. 14:00:01 14:05:00 inclusive). INTERVENTION. data functions default intervention = FALSE, returning market pricing run (INTERVENTION = 0). Pass intervention = TRUE include physical/intervention runs. Types. numeric columns coerced raw character output AEMO CSV files. Timestamps POSIXct. ID columns (regionid, duid, constraintid, etc.) remain character. GST. NEM wholesale prices AEMO’s feeds, therefore every aemo_tbl, exclusive GST (NER Chapter 10, definition “spot price”). comparing revenue figures published financial statements retail prices, gross 1.10 appropriate.

title: Data dictionary: columns, tables, and AEMO source
text: Source tables: - 5-min: DISPATCH_PRICE DISPATCHIS_Reports - 30-min pre-5MS: TRADING_PRICE TRADINGIS_Reports - 30-min post-5MS (1 Oct 2021): aggregated DISPATCH_PRICE rrp column may occasionally equal Market Price Cap (MPC, ~AUD 17,500/MWh) Market Price Floor (MPF, -AUD 1,000/MWh). See aemo_price_caps() exact values financial year.

title: NEM timestamps: AEST, period-ending, and the 5MS transition
text: National Electricity Market operates Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST), UTC+10, year-round. wall-clock time Sydney Melbourne, advance one hour AEDT (UTC+11) Daylight Saving Time (October April year). legal basis NER clause 2.2.6. Using wall-clock time AEMO data analysis common error: every summer timestamp parsed one hour ahead file clock, timestamps DST transition boundaries become ambiguous NA. aemo package uses "Australia/Brisbane" timezone data. Queensland observes AEST year-round (DST), gives correct fixed UTC+10 offset: display times local wall-clock time specific state, convert explicitly:
code: library(aemo)  # All timestamps come back in AEST (UTC+10, no DST). # The tzone attribute is "Australia/Brisbane" throughout. p <- aemo_price("NSW1", "2024-01-15 14:00", "2024-01-15 15:00") attr(p$settlementdate, "tzone")  # "Australia/Brisbane"  # January is summer in Australia. Australia/Sydney would give UTC+11 here, # shifting every timestamp one hour ahead of the AEMO file clock. # Australia/Brisbane correctly gives UTC+10. format(p$settlementdate[1], "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z") # Convert market time to NSW wall clock (observes DST) p$local_time_nsw <- format(p$settlementdate, tz = "Australia/Sydney")  # Convert market time to SA wall clock (UTC+9:30 / UTC+10:30 with DST) p$local_time_sa <- format(p$settlementdate, tz = "Australia/Adelaide")

title: NEM timestamps: AEST, period-ending, and the 5MS transition
text: Every interval NEMweb file labelled period-ending timestamp. row settlementdate = "2024-01-15 14:05:00" covers 5-minute dispatch interval 14:00:01 14:05:00 (inclusive). AEMO convention throughout: timestamp marks interval closes, opens. 30-minute trading intervals rule applies: row stamped 14:30:00 covers 14:00:01 14:30:00. computing time--day statistics (e.g. peak demand hour) use interval end-time directly. joining external time series use period-start timestamps, subtract interval length:
code: # Period-start timestamps for 5-min intervals p$period_start <- p$settlementdate - as.difftime(5, units = "mins")  # Period-start timestamps for 30-min trading intervals t$period_start <- t$settlementdate - as.difftime(30, units = "mins")

title: NEM timestamps: AEST, period-ending, and the 5MS transition
text: 1 October 2021 NEM settled electricity 30-minute trading intervals using TRADINGPRICE TRADINGIS reports. date five-minute settlement (5MS) commenced NER clause 3.15.1A, settlement moved native 5-minute dispatch prices DISPATCHPRICE. Practical implications: constructing long historical 30-minute price series span transition, aggregation method matches: pre-5MS TRADINGPRICE post-5MS arithmetic mean simple unweighted averages six dispatch prices within trading interval (pre-5MS dispatch prices held constant trading-interval price; post-5MS vary).

title: NEM timestamps: AEST, period-ending, and the 5MS transition
text: DISPATCHPRICE contains two types dispatch runs every file: INTERVENTION = 0: market pricing run. price used settlement one analyses want. INTERVENTION = 1: physical/intervention run, published AEMO issued intervention direction (e.g. June 2022 market suspension). runs coexist file. aemo data functions default intervention = FALSE, filters market-pricing rows . Pass intervention = TRUE need analyse intervention pricing (e.g. cost directions suspension period).
code: # Default: market pricing run only p_market <- aemo_price("NSW1", "2022-06-13", "2022-06-14")  # Both runs (for intervention analysis) p_both <- aemo_price("NSW1", "2022-06-13", "2022-06-14",                      intervention = TRUE) table(p_both$intervention)

what: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
text: R package accessing public market data Australian Energy Market Operator. Wholesale electricity prices, regional demand, dispatch, interconnector flows, rooftop PV, generator bids, predispatch forecasts, FCAS markets, gas markets across National Electricity Market.

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
text: Australian Energy Market Operator independent system operator market operator National Electricity Market (NEM), serves eastern southern states Australia, reformed Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM) Western Australia (SCED essential system services markets, go-live 1 October 2023). AEMO also operates Victorian Declared Transmission System Short Term Trading Market gas (Adelaide, Brisbane, Sydney hubs), Wallumbilla Gas Supply Hub, East Coast Gas System functions. Australian Energy Regulator (AER) economic regulator; Australian Energy Market Commission (AEMC) rule maker. NEM one longest interconnected transmission systems world generates around 200 TWh electricity year (2024 calendar year 217 TWh including rooftop PV) across five regions (Queensland, New South Wales plus ACT, Victoria, South Australia, Tasmania). Annual NEM wholesale turnover typically falls AUD 15 25 billion range, 2023-24 AUD 18.6 billion (AER Wholesale Electricity Market Performance Report, December 2024), plus tens hundred million frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) depending volatility. Since 1 October 2021 NEM run 5-minute dispatch settlement cycle (previously 5-minute dispatch 30-minute settlement); every five minutes AEMO publishes prices, demand, generator output, interconnector flows, bids, forecasts. free public. AEMO publishes raw dispatch files NEMweb (http://nemweb.com.au) within minutes dispatch interval, retains historical archives Market Management System Data Model (MMSDM) going back start NEM December 1998. Flagship planning publications (Integrated System Plan (ISP), Electricity Statement Opportunities, Gas Statement Opportunities) released aemo.com.au.

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
text: NEMweb generous unfriendly. 100 report folders. Files zipped CSVs proprietary row-dispatch format (C, comments, , column headers, D, data rows, F, footers) many R-native CSV readers can’t parse. single dispatch file might contain four different tables (prices, demand, interconnector flows, unit output) interleaved one CSV. Monthly archives tables multi-gigabyte. Schema versions change MMSDM releases (v4--v5 transition April 2021, coincident 5-minute settlement program, added columns DISPATCHLOAD). AEMO decommissioning NEMweb HTTP endpoint 7 April 2026 migrating base URL 30 April 2026. NEMweb directory listings parsed tidy data frames. C,/,/D,/F, row-dispatch format handled per-file schema drift transparent. Base URL configurable via options(aemo.base_url = ...) handle April 2026 migration. Rate-limiting caching -box.
code: # Without this package listing <- rvest::read_html("http://nemweb.com.au/Reports/Current/DispatchIS_Reports/") hrefs   <- rvest::html_attr(rvest::html_elements(listing, "a"), "href") zip     <- paste0("http://nemweb.com.au/Reports/Current/DispatchIS_Reports/",                   tail(grep("DISPATCHIS", hrefs, value = TRUE), 1)) path    <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip") download.file(zip, path) tmp     <- tempfile(); utils::unzip(path, exdir = tmp) csv     <- list.files(tmp, pattern = "\\.CSV$", full.names = TRUE)[1] # ... read the file line by line, split by "I,"/"D,"/"F," row type, #     match I headers to D rows, build separate data frames per table, #     validate that schema matches the MMSDM version, ...  # With this package library(aemo) aemo_price("NSW1", "2024-06-01", "2024-06-01 01:00:00")

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
code: library(aemo)  # Reference data (no network required) aemo_regions() aemo_interconnectors() aemo_price_caps()       # MPC / MPF / CPT by financial year  # 5-minute wholesale prices for NSW, last hour p <- aemo_price("NSW1", Sys.time() - 3600, Sys.time()) head(p) # Key columns: settlementdate (POSIXct, AEST), regionid, rrp (AUD/MWh)  # Regional demand (operational) d <- aemo_demand("VIC1", Sys.time() - 3600, Sys.time())  # FCAS prices across all 10 services f <- aemo_fcas("NSW1", Sys.time() - 3600, Sys.time())  # Binding constraints with shadow prices (price-spike forensics) c <- aemo_constraints(Sys.time() - 3600, Sys.time()) head(c[order(-c$marginalvalue), c("settlementdate", "constraintid",                                    "marginalvalue", "rhs")])

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
text: timestamps AEST (UTC+10, fixed year-round) matching AEMO’s market clock (NER clause 2.2.6). Using "Australia/Sydney" silently shift every summer interval one hour. tzone attribute every settlementdate column "Australia/Brisbane". Timestamps period-ending: row stamped 14:05:00 covers 5-minute interval ending 14:05.
code: attr(p$settlementdate, "tzone")  # "Australia/Brisbane"

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
text: DISPATCHPRICE contains two run types every file: market pricing run (INTERVENTION = 0, used settlement) physical/intervention run (INTERVENTION = 1, issued market directions suspension). functions default intervention = FALSE, returning market pricing rows . June 2022 NEM suspension AEMO issued directions across regions several days. Querying period default settings returns market pricing run, direction-influenced run:
code: # Default: market pricing run only (correct for settlement analysis) p_market <- aemo_price("NSW1", "2022-06-13", "2022-06-14")  # Both runs (use for direction-cost analysis) p_both <- aemo_price("NSW1", "2022-06-13", "2022-06-14",                      intervention = TRUE) table(p_both$intervention)  # "0": market run rows, "1": intervention rows

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
code: library(aemo)  # Step 1: find a high-price interval in SA p <- aemo_price("SA1", "2024-03-01", "2024-03-02") spike <- p[which.max(p$rrp), ] spike[, c("settlementdate", "rrp")]  # Step 2: which constraint had the highest shadow price at that time? c <- aemo_constraints(   start = spike$settlementdate - 300,   end   = spike$settlementdate ) head(c[order(-c$marginalvalue), c("constraintid", "marginalvalue", "rhs")])  # Step 3: which generators were providing FCAS at that time? e <- aemo_fcas_enablement(   start = spike$settlementdate - 300,   end   = spike$settlementdate ) # Aggregate raise6secmw across DUIDs to see market-wide enablement if ("raise6secmw" %in% names(e)) {   aggregate(e$raise6secmw, by = list(e$settlementdate), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE) }  # Step 4: what loss factor applies to a generator in SA? mlf <- aemo_mlf(year = "2024-25") head(mlf[mlf$regionid == "SA1", c("duid", "mlf")])

title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
text: AEMO’s bid tables large. BIDPEROFFER_D (per-interval bid price volume bands) monthly archives order 0.5 1.5 GB zipped (much larger uncompressed). naive full-year pull several gigabytes compressed data tens millions rows. aemo_bids() enforces 30-day span guard default refuses longer spans without explicit consent:
code: # Works (1-day span) aemo_bids(duid = "BW01", start = "2024-06-01", end = "2024-06-02")  # Errors with a clear message aemo_bids(duid = "BW01", start = "2024-01-01", end = "2024-06-01") # Error: Requested span is 152 days. # Bids data is large (BIDPEROFFER_D monthly archives are multi-GB zipped). # Pass allow_large = TRUE to proceed.  # Works with explicit opt-in aemo_bids(duid = "BW01", start = "2024-01-01", end = "2024-06-01",           allow_large = TRUE)

what: Regional spot price by hour of day
title: Download Australian Energy Market Operator Data
code: library(aemo)  p <- aemo_price("NSW1",                 start = "2024-06-01",                 end = "2024-06-02")  p$hour <- as.integer(format(as.POSIXct(p$settlementdate), "%H")) avg_by_hour <- aggregate(as.numeric(p$rrp) ~ p$hour, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE) names(avg_by_hour) <- c("hour", "rrp") plot(avg_by_hour, type = "l", xlab = "Hour", ylab = "AUD/MWh")

title: Generator bid stack — aemo_bids
text: Returns BIDDAYOFFER_D (daily bid summary: 10 price bands, MaxAvail, fixed load, locked 12:30 D-1), BIDPEROFFER_D (per-interval availability rebids), two joined (duid, settlementdate, bidtype).

title: Generator bid stack — aemo_bids
code: aemo_bids(   duid,   start,   end,   resolution = c("day", "period", "joined"),   allow_large = FALSE )

title: Generator bid stack — aemo_bids
text: duid Character vector DUIDs. Required. start, end Window. resolution One "day" (default, BIDDAYOFFER_D ), "period" (BIDPEROFFER_D ), "joined" (BIDDAYOFFER_D inner-joined BIDPEROFFER_D (duid, settlementdate, bidtype)). allow_large Logical. Default FALSE.

title: Generator bid stack — aemo_bids
text: aemo_tbl.

title: Generator bid stack — aemo_bids
text: Parent / child structure. BIDDAYOFFER_D carries price bands (priceband1..priceband10), locked 12:30 day ahead rebid. BIDPEROFFER_D carries per-interval availability bands (bandavail1..bandavail10), can rebid intraday. Serious bidding analysis (Goncalves & Menezes 2022 Energy Economics 113 106398; Nelson et al. 2024 AJARE 68(4)) needs joined. Size warning. BIDPEROFFER_D monthly archives multi-gigabyte. default aemo_bids() refuses spans longer 30 days; pass allow_large = TRUE override. Upstream gap. AEMO documented gap BIDPEROFFER_D March 2021 July 2024. Rows range may missing.

title: Generator bid stack — aemo_bids

what: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
title: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
text: Returns DISPATCHCONSTRAINT table NEMweb: one row per binding (near-binding) constraint per 5-minute dispatch interval. row records constraint ID, left-hand side (LHS) right-hand side (RHS) values, marginal value (shadow price constraint AUD/MWh), violation degree .

title: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
code: aemo_constraints(   start,   end,   constraint_id = NULL,   intervention = FALSE,   min_marginal_value = 0.01 )

title: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
text: AEMO NEMweb DISPATCHIS_Reports, DISPATCHCONSTRAINT table.

title: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
text: start, end Window (inclusive), character POSIXct. constraint_id Optional character vector constraint IDs (e.g. "N>>N-NIL_1", "V::S_NIL_TBSE"). NULL returns binding constraints window. intervention Logical. Default FALSE. min_marginal_value Numeric. return constraints marginal value threshold (AUD/MWh). 0 returns every row; default 0.01 filters near-zero shadow prices typically noise.

title: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
text: aemo_tbl columns settlementdate, constraintid, rhs, marginalvalue, violationdegree, lhs, plus intervention flag.

title: Binding transmission and system constraints — aemo_constraints
text: table answers question "RRP high 17:35?": sum marginal values across binding constraints Regional Reference Node equals regional price component attributable network limits. Constraint equations RHS terms (GENCONDATA, GENCONSETINVOKE) published MMSDM separate cadence exposed directly function; use aemo_nemweb_download() MMSDM URL .

title: Regional electricity demand — aemo_demand
text: Returns 5-minute regional demand DISPATCHREGIONSUM. Three demand measures supported, aligned AEMO's Demand Terms taxonomy:

title: Regional electricity demand — aemo_demand
code: aemo_demand(   region,   start,   end,   measure = c("operational", "operational_less_snsg", "native"),   intervention = FALSE )

title: Regional electricity demand — aemo_demand
text: AEMO NEMweb, AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Regional electricity demand — aemo_demand
text: region NEM region code. Vector accepted. start, end Window (inclusive), character POSIXct. measure One "operational" (default), "operational_less_snsg", "native". intervention Logical. Default FALSE filters market pricing runs.

title: Regional electricity demand — aemo_demand
text: aemo_tbl columns settlementdate, regionid, demand_mw (requested measure), plus underlying DISPATCHREGIONSUM columns used derivation.

title: Regional electricity demand — aemo_demand
text: "operational" (default): TOTALDEMAND, grid-measured demand met scheduled semi-scheduled generation plus net interchange. quantity AEMO dispatches. "operational_less_snsg": TOTALDEMAND minus small non-scheduled generation (SS_SOLAR_UIGF + SS_WIND_UIGF present). "native": TOTALDEMAND plus estimated rooftop PV generation. Closest end-use consumption. rooftop PV component available DISPATCHREGIONSUM function warns returns TOTALDEMAND; users join aemo_rooftop_pv() full native-demand estimate. Timestamps AEST (UTC+10, DST).

title: Per-DUID dispatch output — aemo_dispatch_units
text: Returns 5-minute generator output one DUIDs. Three measures available:

title: Per-DUID dispatch output — aemo_dispatch_units
code: aemo_dispatch_units(   duid = NULL,   start,   end,   measure = c("scada_mw", "target_mw", "both") )

title: Per-DUID dispatch output — aemo_dispatch_units
text: duid Optional character vector DUIDs. NULL returns generators. start, end Window (inclusive). measure One "scada_mw" (default), "target_mw", "".

title: Per-DUID dispatch output — aemo_dispatch_units
text: aemo_tbl columns settlementdate, duid, requested measure(s).

title: Per-DUID dispatch output — aemo_dispatch_units
text: "scada_mw" (default): actual metered output DISPATCH_UNIT_SCADA (SCADAVALUE). "target_mw": dispatch target DISPATCHLOAD (TOTALCLEARED). MW AEMO asked unit produce end interval. "": returns SCADAVALUE, INITIALMW (SCADA start interval) TOTALCLEARED (target end) single row per DUID per interval. Use ramp-trajectory research: ramp applied interval straight line INITIALMW TOTALCLEARED. Timestamps AEST (UTC+10, DST).

what: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
title: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
text: Returns Distribution Loss Factor NEM connection points MMSDM LOSSFACTORMODEL table. DLFs measure average energy loss distribution network connection point transmission network boundary; DLF 1.02 means generator must produce 2% energy delivers transmission system.

title: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
code: aemo_dlf(year = NULL, connection_point_id = NULL)

title: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
text: AEMO MMSDM archive, LOSSFACTORMODEL table. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
text: year Financial year(s) "YYYY-YY". NULL returns available years. connection_point_id Optional character vector connection point IDs.

title: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
text: aemo_tbl minimum financial_year, connectionpointid, dlf.

title: Distribution Loss Factors (DLF) by connection point — aemo_dlf
text: DLFs published annually AEMO (NER 3.6.3) combined MLFs settlement give total loss factor (TLF = MLF x DLF) used energy payments.

what: Frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) prices — aemo_fcas
title: Frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) prices — aemo_fcas
text: Returns regional FCAS market prices across eight contingency services plus two regulation services. Ten services live NEM since R1/L1 Fast commenced 9 October 2023.

title: Frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) prices — aemo_fcas
code: aemo_fcas(region, start, end, service = NULL, intervention = FALSE)

title: Frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) prices — aemo_fcas
text: region NEM region code. start, end Window (inclusive). service Optional character vector service names (e.g. "RAISE6SEC", "LOWER60SEC", "RAISE1SEC"). intervention Logical. See aemo_price().

title: Frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) prices — aemo_fcas
text: aemo_tbl one row per interval columns requested FCAS RRP (AUD/MW).

title: Frequency control ancillary services (FCAS) prices — aemo_fcas
text: Thin wrapper aemo_price(..., market = "fcas").

what: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
title: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
text: Returns MW enabled ten Frequency Control Ancillary Services (FCAS) per DUID per 5-minute dispatch interval DISPATCHLOAD. quantity side FCAS market: much unit enabled (dispatched) provide service, distinct price returned aemo_fcas().

title: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
code: aemo_fcas_enablement(   duid = NULL,   start,   end,   service = NULL,   intervention = FALSE )

title: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
text: AEMO NEMweb DISPATCHIS_Reports, DISPATCHLOAD table.

title: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
text: duid Optional character vector DUIDs. NULL returns units. See aemo_units() full DUID registry. start, end Window (inclusive), character POSIXct. service Optional character vector service names, e.g. c("raise6sec", "lowerreg"). Case-insensitive. NULL returns ten services. intervention Logical. Default FALSE.

title: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
text: aemo_tbl columns settlementdate, duid, one column per FCAS service (raise1secmw, lower1secmw, raise6secmw, lower6secmw, raise60secmw, lower60secmw, raise5minmw, lower5minmw, raiseregmw, lowerregmw). Values MW; zero means unit enabled service.

title: FCAS enablement volumes by generator unit — aemo_fcas_enablement
text: Ten services active since 9 October 2023 Fast (RAISE1SEC / LOWER1SEC) commenced. date eight services present; raise1secmw / lower1secmw columns NA intervals date.

what: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
text: Downloads GENCONDATA table recent MMSDM monthly archive. GENCONDATA contains equation definitions every generic constraint active NEM: constraint ID, type (equality/inequality), description constraint models (thermal limit, voltage stability, system strength, etc.), default RHS value.

title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
code: aemo_gencon(constraint_id = NULL, type = NULL)

title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, GENCONDATA table. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
text: constraint_id Optional character vector constraint IDs filter . NULL returns equations. type Optional constraint type filter (e.g. "LE" <=, "GE" >=, "EQ" =). NULL returns types.

title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
text: aemo_tbl columns including genconid (constraint ID), constrainttype, description, genericconstraintrhs (default RHS value). Additional columns GENCONDATA (effective dates, generic constraint equation weights) present available.

title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon
text: Pair aemo_constraints() go binding dispatch interval underlying network equation. workflow : aemo_constraints() tells constraint bound hard (marginalvalue = shadow price); aemo_gencon() tells constraint (elements RHS set level).

title: Generic constraint equations and RHS terms (GENCONDATA) — aemo_gencon

title: NEM interconnectors — aemo_interconnectors

title: NEM interconnectors — aemo_interconnectors
code: aemo_interconnectors()

title: NEM interconnectors — aemo_interconnectors
text: aemo_tbl columns interconnector_id, from_region, to_region, name, energised.

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
text: Returns MARKETNOTICEDATA feed MMSDM. Market notices AEMO's free-text log market-relevant events: Lack Reserve (LOR1/2/3) declarations, Reliability Emergency Reserve Trader (RERT) activations, market suspensions, market directions, unit withdrawals, system security events, administered price declarations, operator advisories.

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
code: aemo_market_notices(start, end, notice_type = NULL, region = NULL)

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, MARKETNOTICEDATA table. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
text: start, end Window (inclusive) applied EFFECTIVEDATE. notice_type Optional character vector (e.g. "LOR1", "RERT", "PRICES SUBJECT REVIEW"). Case-insensitive substring match. region Optional NEM region code.

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
text: aemo_tbl columns MARKETNOTICEDATA: noticeid, effectivedate, typeid (notice category), originator, priority, reason (notice text), externalreference, present regionid.

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
text: Pair aemo_constraints() aemo_price() sequence causal chain price event. Rangarajan, Svec, Foley Trück (2025, Energy Economics 141) use MARKETNOTICEDATA order intervention messages mark entry exit June 2022 NEM suspension.

title: NEM market notices — aemo_market_notices
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(aemo.cache_dir = tempdir()) try({   # LOR declarations during the June 2022 NEM suspension   n <- aemo_market_notices(     start = "2022-06-13",     end   = "2022-06-14",     notice_type = "LOR"   )   head(n) }) #> ℹ Downloading <https://nemweb.com.au/Data_Archive/Wholesale_Electricity/MMSDM/2… #> ✖ Downloading <https://nemweb.com.au/Data_Archive/Wholesale_Electricity/MMSDM/2… #>  #> Error in aemo_market_notices(start = "2022-06-13", end = "2022-06-14",  :  #>   Could not retrieve MARKETNOTICEDATA from MMSDM. #> ℹ Requested months: "2022-06". Check your connection or try #>   `aemo_nemweb_download()` with an MMSDM URL. options(op) # }

what: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
text: Returns Marginal Loss Factor applicable DUID requested financial year(s). MLFs measure incremental network loss connection point relative Regional Reference Node (RRN); DUID MLF = 0.97 receives 97% regional RRP per MWh generated.

title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
code: aemo_mlf(year = NULL, duid = NULL)

title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
text: AEMO MMSDM archive, TRANSMISSIONLOSSFACTOR table. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
text: year Financial year(s) "YYYY-YY" strings (e.g. "2024-25"). NULL returns available years. Multiple years accepted. duid Optional character vector DUIDs filter . NULL returns DUIDs. See aemo_units() full DUID registry.

title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
text: aemo_tbl columns financial_year, duid, connectionpointid, regionid, mlf. live MMSDM download additional columns (participantid, lastchanged) may also present.

title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
text: MLFs published annually AEMO NER 3.6.2 used settlement calculations PPA revenue reconstruction. function first attempts download TRANSMISSIONLOSSFACTOR table recent MMSDM monthly archive; fails returns bundled static table covering FY 2020-21 FY 2025-26 ~20 well-known DUIDs.

title: Marginal Loss Factors (MLF) by DUID and financial year — aemo_mlf
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(aemo.cache_dir = tempdir()) try({   mlf <- aemo_mlf(year = "2024-25")   head(mlf) }) #> ℹ Downloading <https://nemweb.com.au/Data_Archive/Wholesale_Electricity/MMSDM/2… #> ✔ Downloading <https://nemweb.com.au/Data_Archive/Wholesale_Electricity/MMSDM/2… #>  #> Error in aemo_mlf(year = "2024-25") :  #>   Could not retrieve TRANSMISSIONLOSSFACTOR from MMSDM. #> ℹ MLFs are published annually by AEMO under NER 3.6.2. For historical analysis, #>   download the determination PDF directly from aemo.com.au, or retry when the #>   MMSDM archive is reachable. #> ℹ No fallback is provided: unsourced MLF values would be a correctness trap in #>   settlement and PPA workflows. options(op) # }

what: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
title: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
text: Returns NETWORK_OUTAGEDETAIL table MMSDM, records every planned forced outage NEM transmission distribution network elements. Outages primary driver binding constraints price spikes: line transformer service network constrained, reducing thermal limits appear RHS values DISPATCHCONSTRAINT.

title: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
code: aemo_outages(start, end, element_id = NULL, outage_type = NULL, region = NULL)

title: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, NETWORK_OUTAGEDETAIL table. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
text: start, end Outage window (inclusive). Filters starttime endtime: outage active window returned. Character POSIXct. element_id Optional character vector network element IDs. NULL returns elements. outage_type Optional character. One "PLANNED", "FORCED", NULL (). Case-insensitive. region Optional NEM region code. Filters region column available.

title: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
text: aemo_tbl columns NETWORK_OUTAGEDETAIL including outageid, starttime, endtime, substationid, equipmenttype, equipmentid, outagetype, regionid, restarttimeunknown. Exact column set depends MMSDM version.

title: Planned and forced network outages — aemo_outages
text: Use case. Pair aemo_constraints() explain price spike: aemo_outages() tells elements -service time; aemo_constraints() tells constraints bound; together answer "SA spot price AUD 15,000 17:35?".

what: NEM market participants and their registered DUIDs — aemo_participants
title: NEM market participants and their registered DUIDs — aemo_participants
text: Returns mapping NEM market participants (companies) registered Dispatchable Unit Identifiers (DUIDs), joined MMSDM PARTICIPANT DUDETAILSUMMARY tables. Use corporate ownership analysis: rolling generator output bids DUID-level data company level.

title: NEM market participants and their registered DUIDs — aemo_participants
code: aemo_participants()

title: NEM market participants and their registered DUIDs — aemo_participants
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, PARTICIPANT DUDETAILSUMMARY tables. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: NEM market participants and their registered DUIDs — aemo_participants
text: aemo_tbl columns participantid, participantclassid (e.g. GENERATOR, LOAD, TRADER), name (company name), duid, stationid, regionid, dispatchtype, schedule_type. Rows one per participant-DUID combination. MMSDM unreachable, returns empty table warning.

what: Projected Assessment of System Adequacy (PASA) — aemo_pasa
title: Projected Assessment of System Adequacy (PASA) — aemo_pasa
text: Returns short-term (STPASA, 1-7 day) medium-term (MTPASA, 2-year) system adequacy projections.

title: Projected Assessment of System Adequacy (PASA) — aemo_pasa
code: aemo_pasa(   horizon = c("short", "medium"),   region = NULL,   start = NULL,   end = NULL )

title: Projected Assessment of System Adequacy (PASA) — aemo_pasa
text: horizon One "short" (default) "medium". region Optional NEM region code. start, end Optional window run-times. Defaults last 24 hours.

title: Projected Assessment of System Adequacy (PASA) — aemo_pasa
text: aemo_tbl.

what: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch
title: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch
text: Returns AEMO's forecast prices demand NEM region. Two horizons: "p5min": 5-minute-ahead forecast, 12 intervals ahead, published every 5 minutes. "predispatch": 40-hour-ahead predispatch 30-minute resolution, published every 30 minutes.

title: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch
code: aemo_predispatch(   region,   start,   end,   horizon = c("predispatch", "p5min"),   run_datetime = NULL )

title: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch
text: region NEM region code. start, end Window forecast run-times. horizon One "predispatch" (default) "p5min". run_datetime Optional character POSIXct. specific RUN_DATETIME pin . NULL (default) returns every vintage issued [start, end].

title: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch
text: aemo_tbl.

title: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch
text: 7-day predispatch publication retired 5-minute settlement commenced; longer horizons use aemo_pasa(). Vintages. PREDISPATCH P5MIN forecasts re-issued every 30 5 minutes respectively. Every vintage archived RUN_DATETIME. default (run_datetime = NULL) function returns vintages whose file timestamp falls [start, end], .e. forecasts issued window. periodid / datetime columns returned rows give forecast's target time. forecast-error research (comparing forecast vintage realised dispatch) pass run_datetime pin specific vintage:   vintage-aware pattern used Prakash (2023) NEMSEER (JOSS 8(92) 5883).
code: # The PREDISPATCH run issued at 15:00 on 1 June 2024 -- # 80 half-hour-ahead rows covering 15:30 out to 31:30 hours. v <- aemo_predispatch("NSW1", start = "2024-06-01", end = "2024-06-02",                       run_datetime = "2024-06-01 15:00")

title: Price and demand forecasts (P5MIN, PREDISPATCH) — aemo_predispatch

title: Wholesale electricity prices — aemo_price
text: Returns 5-minute dispatch prices 30-minute trading prices NEM region specified window. Filters intervention runs default returned prices market clearing prices used settlement.

title: Wholesale electricity prices — aemo_price
code: aemo_price(   region,   start,   end,   interval = c("5min", "30min"),   market = c("energy", "fcas"),   intervention = FALSE )

title: Wholesale electricity prices — aemo_price
text: AEMO NEMweb http://nemweb.com.au, AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Wholesale electricity prices — aemo_price
text: region One "NSW1", "QLD1", "SA1", "TAS1", "VIC1". Accepts vector. start, end Start end times (inclusive). Character (parsed AEST) POSIXct. interval One "5min" (default) "30min". market One "energy" (default, returns RRP) "fcas" (returns FCAS service RRPs). intervention Logical. FALSE (default) returns market pricing run; TRUE returns market physical runs, intervention column preserved.

title: Wholesale electricity prices — aemo_price
text: aemo_tbl. Key columns include settlementdate (POSIXct AEST), regionid, rrp (AUD/MWh, energy) FCAS service RRPs (AUD/MW), intervention.

title: Wholesale electricity prices — aemo_price
text: Timestamps AEST (UTC+10, daylight savings) match AEMO's market clock. See package-level documentation period-ending timestamp convention (row stamped 00:05 5-minute period ending 00:05). Intervention. DISPATCHPRICE contains market pricing runs (INTERVENTION = 0) physical / intervention runs (INTERVENTION = 1). default filters market runs. Pass intervention = TRUE get . 30-minute settlement 5MS transition. 1 October 2021 NEM settled 30-minute trading prices TRADINGPRICE (TRADINGIS). 1 October 2021 five-minute settlement (5MS) commenced settlement moved native 5-minute prices. interval = "30min": pre-5MS period (start < 2021-10-01): prices read TRADINGIS (TRADINGPRICE). post-5MS period: prices derived taking arithmetic mean six 5-minute dispatch prices within 30-minute trading interval, consistent AEMO calculates TRADINGPRICE column TradingIS post-5MS. Data availability. NEMweb Current-directory files retain last ~30 days 5-minute dispatch files. Historical queries use Archive daily-rollup files automatically; queries older Archive window, use aemo_nemweb_download() MMSDM URL directly.

what: NEM market price caps, price floor, and CPT by financial year — aemo_price_caps
title: NEM market price caps, price floor, and CPT by financial year — aemo_price_caps
text: Returns static reference table Market Price Cap (MPC), Market Price Floor (MPF), Cumulative Price Threshold (CPT), Administered Price Cap (APC) apply NEM financial year (1 July 30 June). set AEMC NER 3.9.4 indexed annually CPI.

title: NEM market price caps, price floor, and CPT by financial year — aemo_price_caps
code: aemo_price_caps()

title: NEM market price caps, price floor, and CPT by financial year — aemo_price_caps
text: aemo_tbl columns year (financial year, "YYYY-YY"), market_price_cap_aud_per_mwh, market_price_floor_aud_per_mwh, cumulative_price_threshold_aud, administered_price_cap_aud_per_mwh, source.

title: NEM market price caps, price floor, and CPT by financial year — aemo_price_caps
text: Use table interpret spot-price extremes: RRP hitting MPC (typically AUD 15,000 17,500 per MWh depending year) indicates price cap event; rolling-seven-day cumulative price region exceeds CPT (~AUD 1.5 million per MWh-equivalent), AEMO imposes APC (AUD 300/MWh) CPT falls back threshold. table covers 2015-16 onwards updated package release. authoritative current values see https://www.aemc.gov.au/regulation/energy-rules/national-electricity-rules AEMO Reliability Settings publications.

title: NEM market price caps, price floor, and CPT by financial year — aemo_price_caps
code: caps <- aemo_price_caps() head(caps) #> # aemo_tbl: NEM market price caps, floor, CPT, and APC #> # Source:   https://www.aemc.gov.au/regulation/energy-rules/national-electricity-rules #> # Licence:  AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice #> # Retrieved: 2026-04-27 21:37 UTC  #> # Rows: 6  Cols: 6 #>  #>      year market_price_cap_aud_per_mwh market_price_floor_aud_per_mwh #> 1 2015-16                        13800                          -1000 #> 2 2016-17                        14000                          -1000 #> 3 2017-18                        14200                          -1000 #> 4 2018-19                        14500                          -1000 #> 5 2019-20                        14700                          -1000 #> 6 2020-21                        15000                          -1000 #>   cumulative_price_threshold_aud administered_price_cap_aud_per_mwh #> 1                         212800                                300 #> 2                         216500                                300 #> 3                         219700                                300 #> 4                         224300                                300 #> 5                         228700                                300 #> 6                         232900                                300 #>                                                  source_doc #> 1 AEMC 2014 RSSR final determination (CPI-indexed annually) #> 2 AEMC 2014 RSSR final determination (CPI-indexed annually) #> 3 AEMC 2014 RSSR final determination (CPI-indexed annually) #> 4 AEMC 2018 RSSR final determination (CPI-indexed annually) #> 5 AEMC 2018 RSSR final determination (CPI-indexed annually) #> 6 AEMC 2018 RSSR final determination (CPI-indexed annually)

what: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
text: Returns settlement reconciliation tables MMSDM. Three views exposed, corresponding three tables gentailer hedging workflows commonly need:

title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
code: aemo_settlement(table = c("cashflow", "fcas_recovery", "residues"), start, end)

title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, SETCFM / SETFCASREGIONRECOVERY / SETRESIDUECONTRACTPAYMENT. AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
text: table One "cashflow", "fcas_recovery", "residues". start, end Window (inclusive) applied SETTLEMENTDATE / PAYMENTDATE.

title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
text: aemo_tbl raw MMSDM columns requested table.

title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
text: "cashflow" (default): SETCFM (NEMDE-derived energy settlement amounts per participant per trading interval). "fcas_recovery": SETFCASREGIONRECOVERY (recovery allocation FCAS costs customer load per region per trading interval). "residues": SETRESIDUECONTRACTPAYMENT (settlement residue auction (SRA) contract payments interconnector settlement residues). Access. tables MMSDM monthly archive (NEMweb Current retention). Expect two-month latency trading date availability.

title: Settlement cash-flow and residue tables — aemo_settlement
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(aemo.cache_dir = tempdir()) try({   s <- aemo_settlement(table = "cashflow",                         start = "2024-06-01",                         end   = "2024-06-02")   head(s) }) #> ℹ Downloading <https://nemweb.com.au/Data_Archive/Wholesale_Electricity/MMSDM/2… #> ✖ Downloading <https://nemweb.com.au/Data_Archive/Wholesale_Electricity/MMSDM/2… #>  #> Error in aemo_settlement(table = "cashflow", start = "2024-06-01", end = "2024-06-02") :  #>   Could not retrieve SETCFM from MMSDM. #> ℹ Settlement tables are published with ~2 month latency. Check that "2024-06" #>   is within the published window. options(op) # }

what: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
title: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
text: Returns one-row-per-source provenance record aemo_tbl, suitable inclusion paper appendix, Zenodo deposit, CRAN-style data manifest. snapshot captures:

title: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
code: aemo_snapshot(x)

title: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
text: x aemo_tbl (list ).

title: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
text: data frame one row per table.

title: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
text: title: human-readable title table; source: NEMweb / MMSDM URL family table drawn ; licence: AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice (always); retrieved: POSIXct timestamp table constructed; rows, cols: observed dimensions; sha256: SHA-256 digest table's printed body, stable across R versions platforms. sha256 column makes snapshot pinnable: query returns different hash, underlying data changed (row-order , also worth knowing). Pair git commit analysis script give reader closed reproducibility loop.

title: Snapshot provenance for an aemo_tbl — aemo_snapshot
code: x <- structure(   data.frame(settlementdate = Sys.time(), region = "NSW1", rrp = 80),   aemo_title = "Demo",   aemo_source = "http://nemweb.com.au",   aemo_licence = "AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice",   aemo_retrieved = Sys.time(),   class = c("aemo_tbl", "data.frame") ) aemo_snapshot(x) #>   title               source                           licence #> 1  Demo http://nemweb.com.au AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice #>             retrieved rows cols #> 1 2026-04-27 21:37:02    1    3 #>                                                             sha256 #> 1 70d1a5a1abe1e17af54c5105884f212c1a4cd0a4d6814d92fd53f711ff333726

what: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
title: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
text: Returns SPD (Security Projected Dispatch) constraint coefficient tables MMSDM. GENCONDATA gives high-level equation definition, SPD tables give per-term coefficients used NEMDE dispatch optimisation:

title: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
code: aemo_spd_constraints(   table = c("region", "interconnector", "connection_point"),   constraint_id = NULL )

title: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, SPDREGIONCONSTRAINT / SPDINTERCONNECTORCONSTRAINT / SPDCONNECTIONPOINTCONSTRAINT.

title: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
text: table One "region" (default), "interconnector", "connection_point". constraint_id Optional character vector GENCONID/CONSTRAINTID values.

title: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
text: aemo_tbl columns requested SPD table. GENCONID FACTOR (coefficient) always present; columns vary table.

title: SPD constraint tables (regions, interconnectors, connection points) — aemo_spd_constraints
text: "region": SPDREGIONCONSTRAINT (regional-demand regional-generation terms). "interconnector": SPDINTERCONNECTORCONSTRAINT (interconnector-flow terms). "connection_point": SPDCONNECTIONPOINTCONSTRAINT (per-DUID connection-point terms). tables required NEM dispatch replication (nempy Python package, Gorman, Bruce & MacGill 2022, JOSS 7(70) 3596, doi:10.21105/joss.03596, uses three reproducing NEMDE solves).

title: Configure request throttling — aemo_throttle
text: Controls delay successive NEMweb requests. Defaults 1 second (half request per second).

title: Configure request throttling — aemo_throttle
code: aemo_throttle(delay = 1)

title: Configure request throttling — aemo_throttle
text: delay Numeric. Minimum delay requests seconds. Internally becomes httr2::req_throttle(rate = 1/delay).

title: Configure request throttling — aemo_throttle
text: Invisibly returns previous value.

title: Configure request throttling — aemo_throttle
code: old <- aemo_throttle(0.5)  # 2 requests per second aemo_throttle(old)         # restore

title: NEM generators (DUID registry) — aemo_units
text: Downloads DUDETAILSUMMARY table recent MMSDM monthly archive returns one row per registered DUID (Dispatchable Unit Identifier) station, region, dispatch type, classification, schedule type. Typical output 500+ DUIDs covering scheduled, semi-scheduled, non-scheduled generators, bidirectional storage (BESS), loads.

title: NEM generators (DUID registry) — aemo_units
code: aemo_units(as_of = NULL)

title: NEM generators (DUID registry) — aemo_units
text: AEMO NEMweb MMSDM archive, DUDETAILSUMMARY table, AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice.

title: NEM generators (DUID registry) — aemo_units
text: as_of Optional Date POSIXct. Returns DUID registry date. NULL (default) returns current registry.

title: NEM generators (DUID registry) — aemo_units
text: aemo_tbl keyed duid. Columns include (DUDETAILSUMMARY): duid, stationid, regionid, dispatchtype (GENERATOR / LOAD), connectionpointid, schedule_type (SCHEDULED / SEMI-SCHEDULED / NON-SCHEDULED), start_date, end_date.

title: NEM generators (DUID registry) — aemo_units
text: Effective-date filtering. DUDETAILSUMMARY effective-dated (START_DATE, END_DATE per row multiple VERSIONNO vintages per DUID time). default (as_of = NULL) returns rows whose [START_DATE, END_DATE] interval covers date MMSDM archive published: .e. current registry. Pass as_of date get registry date (essential historical analysis: Liddell's four DUIDs retired April 2023; pre-2023 queries need ). matches --join pattern documented Gorman et al. (2018) NEMOSIS (APSRC) correct historical joins.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: First release new maintainership. Reclaims archived aemo package name (Imanuel Costigan, 2014-2020, v0.1.0 v0.3.0, archived 2021-12-29). prior package shipped three functions covering regional prices demand ; release independent rewrite covering full NEMweb MMSDM data surface shares package name. Version starts 0.4.0 sit strictly last archived version (0.3.0).

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_price(): 5-min (DISPATCHPRICE) 30-min (TRADINGPRICE) regional wholesale prices. Pre-5MS (2021-10-01) reads TRADINGIS; post-5MS 30-min prices derived arithmetic mean six 5-min dispatch prices within trading interval, matching AEMO’s post-5MS TRADINGPRICE derivation. aemo_demand(): regional operational, operational-less-SNSG, native demand 5-min resolution. aemo_price_caps(): static reference table Market Price Cap, Floor, CPT, APC financial year. Per-row source citations specific AEMC determination.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_dispatch_units(): per-DUID generator output 5-min. measure = "scada_mw" (default) returns SCADA actual; "target_mw" returns DISPATCHLOAD TOTALCLEARED; "" returns INITIALMW + TOTALCLEARED + SCADAVALUE paired, enabling ramp-trajectory research. aemo_interconnector(): NEM interconnector flows. aemo_rooftop_pv(): estimated rooftop PV actual forecast. aemo_fcas(), aemo_fcas_enablement(): FCAS prices enablement across ten services live since 9 Oct 2023. aemo_constraints(): binding transmission system constraints shadow prices. aemo_gencon(): GENCONDATA constraint equations. aemo_spd_constraints(): SPD constraint coefficient tables (SPDREGIONCONSTRAINT, SPDINTERCONNECTORCONSTRAINT, SPDCONNECTIONPOINTCONSTRAINT). Required nempy-style dispatch replication (Gorman et al. 2022 JOSS 7(70) 3596). aemo_outages(): NETWORK_OUTAGEDETAIL. aemo_market_notices(): MARKETNOTICEDATA feed (LOR, RERT, interventions, suspensions). Pairs market- event forensics (Rangarajan et al. 2025, Energy Economics 141).

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_bids(): generator bid stack. resolution = "day" (BIDDAYOFFER_D), "period" (BIDPEROFFER_D), "joined" (two inner-joined (duid, settlementdate, bidtype) price bands volumes accessible together). aemo_predispatch(): P5MIN PREDISPATCH forecasts. Pass run_datetime pin specific forecast vintage (required forecast-error research; mirrors NEMSEER’s workflow, Prakash 2023 JOSS 8(92) 5883). aemo_pasa(): short-term medium-term projected assessment system adequacy. Default windows horizon-aware.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_gas(): STTM DWGM.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_units(): DUID registry. as_of argument performs effective-date join DUDETAILSUMMARY historical queries see registry point time (e.g. Liddell’s DUIDs pre-2023 retirement). aemo_participants(): PARTICIPANT + DUDETAILSUMMARY. aemo_regions(), aemo_interconnectors(): static metadata. aemo_mlf(), aemo_dlf(): marginal distribution loss factors MMSDM. aemo_settlement(): SETCFM (energy cashflow), SETFCASREGIONRECOVERY (FCAS recovery), SETRESIDUECONTRACTPAYMENT (settlement residues) gentailer reconciliation workflows.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_snapshot(): returns provenance record (source URL, SHA-256 table body, row count, retrieved-timestamp) suitable Zenodo deposit paper appendix.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: aemo_nemweb_ls(), aemo_nemweb_download(): escape hatches arbitrary NEMweb paths. aemo_cache_info(), aemo_clear_cache(), aemo_throttle().

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: timestamps POSIXct Australia/Brisbane (AEST, UTC+10, DST). NER cl. 2.2.6. Using Australia/Sydney silently shift every summer interval one hour. Period-ending timestamps throughout. intervention = FALSE (default) filters DISPATCHPRICE market pricing run (INTERVENTION = 0) used settlement. Pass intervention = TRUE intervention runs (e.g. June 2022 NEM suspension). options("aemo.base_url") options("aemo.mmsdm_base") override NEMweb host endpoints, supporting 30 April 2026 NEMweb URL migration without reinstall.

title: aemo 0.4.0
text: Data published AEMO http://nemweb.com.au AEMO Copyright Permissions Notice https://www.aemo.com.au/privacy--legal-notices/copyright-permissions. Downloads cached tools::R_user_dir("aemo", "cache") first use, SHA-256 content verification reuse.
